title: Marsdenia inodora
taxonomy: Marsdenia inodora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Marsdenia is a group of tropical vines and shrubs that are native to Australia and the Pacific Islands. Marsdenia inodora is specifically a species of flowering vine that is native to New South Wales in Australia. The classification of Marsdenia inodora is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Gentianales, Family: Apocynaceae, Genus: Marsdenia, Species: M. inodora. Marsdenia inodora is a unique and fascinating species within the Marsdenia genus.

The health benefits of Marsdenia inodora include its potential to alleviate inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-cancer properties.
